title: US Media Compares Trump Mass Deportations to Cold War-Era Latin American Disappearances
detail: Major US media outlets including WBEZ Chicago and the Chicago Sun-Times published significant analyses on April 10–12, 2026, drawing direct comparisons between the Trump administration's mass deportation operations and the forced disappearances practiced by US-backed Latin American military dictatorships during the 1970s and 1980s. The coverage drew on ICE records showing 2,253 deportation flights to 79 countries in the first year of the second Trump administration—a 46% increase over the previous year—and a February 2026 federal court ruling that deportation of migrants to third countries not their home countries was unlawful. Critics noted the historical irony: the US was mass-deporting people from countries whose instability was in part created by US military and CIA interventions decades earlier, while employing rhetoric that echoed the 'national security' justifications used by the US-backed Dirty War governments it once supported.

Chicago Sun-Times: Trump's Deportation Blitz Evokes Painful Memories of Latin American Disappearances
